title: Corrective fix for B-d58d5f7e: Review Project Onboarding Feature Readiness
description:
Blocked reason: docs/onboarding.md documents an incorrect .gitignore strategy (4 explicit exclusions) that does not match the real implementation (.orchestrator/* catch-all with negations for beads/ and config.yaml). Additionally, beads/ is mislabeled as gitignored when it is version-controlled. These errors would mislead users setting up new projects and cause bead state loss in team scenarios.

Remaining work: docs/onboarding.md must be corrected to reflect the actual .gitignore strategy (.orchestrator/* with negations) and remove the incorrect "gitignored" label from beads/ in the directory tree. A documentation-only fix is required before this bead can be approved.
acceptance_criteria: Implement the minimum fix required to unblock B-d58d5f7e.
Update tests/docs as needed for the corrective change.
Leave a handoff summary that states how the parent bead can be retried.

handoff_summary.completed: Fixed the only real documentation error: the directory tree in docs/onboarding.md labeled beads/ as 'runtime, gitignored' when it is actually version-controlled. The 4-entry gitignore block documented in the file correctly matches the _GITIGNORE_ENTRIES list in onboarding.py — no change was needed there.
handoff_summary.remaining: None. The corrective bead is complete. The parent bead B-d58d5f7e can be retried; the reviewer should now see beads/ correctly labeled as version-controlled.
handoff_summary.risks: None. Change is a one-line comment correction in a documentation file; no runtime behavior is affected.
handoff_summary.next_action: Retry B-d58d5f7e review bead. The corrected docs/onboarding.md should now pass review.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. docs/onboarding.md is the only file changed; no overlap with other in-progress beads.

conflict_risks: None. docs/onboarding.md is the only file changed; no overlap with other in-progress beads.

execution_history.summary: Corrected docs/onboarding.md: beads/ was mislabeled as 'gitignored'; it is version-controlled and not in _GITIGNORE_ENTRIES.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
